area_anchor: PLAT Claude tool selection - no explicit read-first protocol
current_truth: Repo guidance says to read before editing, but there is no short canonical tool ladder that tells Claude when to use Read, Glob, Grep, MCP language tools, or Bash. That leaves tool choice implicit and inconsistent.
next_scope_now: Fact-find the correct Claude tool order and decision boundaries: Read for file contents, Glob for discovery, Grep for search, MCP tools for semantic TypeScript work, and Bash only for execution or guarded repo commands. Then map the rule into the right docs and permission surfaces.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: we should ensure the correct tool calls are made and define what those tool calls should be, for example Read, Glob, Grep, and Bash
agent-verified: docs/INDEX_FOR_CLAUDE.md and .claude/SETUP_COMPLETE.md already say to read before editing
agent-verified: docs/ide/agent-language-intelligence-guide.md already defines MCP-first behavior for TypeScript diagnostics
why: The tool policy is currently underspecified, so Claude falls back to Bash too often. A clear tool ladder should reduce errors and make sessions more deterministic.

intended_outcome.statement: Claude-facing guidance defines a canonical tool-selection ladder and the related permission model so discovery and semantic work happen without unnecessary shell calls.
